Using the pulse setting on the blender, pulse several times until blended and pour into large bowl.
Use the remainder of the ingredients and pulse in the blender. Add to the rest of your salsa in large bowl.
Cut limes in half and squeeze into salsa.
Add pepper, salt, and garlic powder (unless you are using fresh garlic) to taste and mix well.
Add fresh cilantro to taste (optional).

name: Salsa-In-Law
directions: Roast jalapenos first, when blackened put in plastic bag to steam.Set aside for now. Chop onions & cilantro put in bowl. Add tomatoes to blender/processor bowl.
Peel jalapenos remove as many seeds as desired chop roughly add to tomatoes Pulse 2 times. Just enough to chop up the tomato and mix the two together.
Add to bowl mix, add garlic salt to taste.
Allow to sit in fridge at least 2 hrs for it all to marry.
updated: May 10, 2011 My family wanted it hotter so I now add 2 jalapeno's and 1 Serrano.
Be sure to wear gloves when handling hot peppers.

name: Blender Hollandaise Sauce With Garlic
directions: Melt butter until very hot, but not browned.
In a blender at high speed blend egg yolks, lemon juice, salt, cayenne and garlic until yolks are frothy.
Turn blender to low speed and slowly pour in hot butter in a steady stream.
Blend around 15 seconds until the sauce is creamy.

name: Chimichurri Sauce
directions: Puree all ingredients in a blender until smooth.

name: Sweet and Tangy BBQ Sauce
directions: Measure all the ingredients into a small saucepan. Heat over a low heat until the sugar is dissolved and the sauce begins to bubble slightly, stirring occasionally. Remove from the heat and cool completely before refrigerating. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

name: Chili's Honey Chipotle Sauce
directions: Mix all ingredients together and microwave on high for 1 - 1 1/2 minutes.  Let cool to let flavors meld.

name: Mustard Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients except butter.  Cook over medium - low heat until thick, about 15 minutes.  Stirring constantly.   When thickened, remove from heat and add butter.  Stir till melted and serve.

similar.name: Susan's Sweet Mustard Sauce for Ham

name: Homemade Honey Garlic Sauce
directions: mix above ingredients.
add cornstarch if you desire a thicker sauce. Heat until thickened.
serve with stirfry's, meat dishes, etc.

similar.name: Quick and Easy Honey Garlic Sauce

name: White Barbecue Sauce
directions: Whisk all ingredients together until blended. Store in the refrigerator up to 1 week.

name: Chick-Fil-A Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients together except the orange juice and stir well. Thin to pouring with orange juice. Store in refrigerator.

similar.name: Honey Mustard Salad Dressing or Sauce
Super Easy Honey Mustard Sauce (Or Salad Dressing)

name: Susan's Sweet Mustard Sauce for Ham
directions: Combine all ingredients (beat eggs first).
Cook on low heat (3 or 4 to start).
Whisk about every 2 minutes until thickened.
Don't be tempted to cook on too high a heat as it will cause the eggs to curdle.

similar.name: Super Easy Honey Mustard Sauce (Or Salad Dressing)

name: Memphis BBQ Sauce
directions: Add all ingredients and simmer over medium low heat for 20 minutes stirring occasionally until smooth and hot.

name: Honey Dijon Sauce
directions: Mix and chill.

similar.name: Super Easy Honey Mustard Sauce (Or Salad Dressing)

name: Sweet Baby Mark's Barbeque Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients in a saucepan. Simmer over low heat 15 minutes.

Note: I never use measuring cups or spoons, I just kind of eyeball it. This recipe is hard to mess up. Use more brown sugar & honey if you like it sweeter, use less if you don't. Use more hot sauce if you like it hotter, less if you don't. You can play around with it as much as you like. Also, when I first followed the original recipe, it was too thin. I ended up using a rue (flour & warm water blended),  slowly adding to sauce while cooking until I got the thickness I wanted.

Option: Instead of using garlic powder, I would suggest using 2-4 cloves of fresh minced garlic. If you go with the fresh garlic, I would also suggest Sautee-ing the garlic and onion in the butter for about 6 minutes before adding the remaining ingredients. It adds a little time to your prep time, but I think it would bring out a TON of flavor & be well worth the 6 extra minutes. Also, it wouldn't hurt to throw in a dash of smoke flavoring (I didn't because I didn't have any & didn't want to run to the store that late at night).  If you like, you can mince up some green or red pepper, or use chicken or beef broth instead of water. This is just a base recipe, you can use it as instructed or add your own "flair!" Bon Appetit!

name: Cheese and Mustard Steak Sauce
directions: Heat milk in a pan.
Add a little water to the cornflour in a mug; stir until it is a thin paste.
Add cornflour mixture, while constantly stirring, into the warm milk.
When the sauce has thickened to your preference add the cheese to the pan, and mix until cheese is melted through.
Just before serving stir in the mustard.
Enjoy!

name: Salsa With a Zing
directions: Place the salsa in a fine sieve and remove about 2-3 tablespoons of liquid.
Place in a bowl, add the juice, coriander and salt - stir.
Let sit for a couple of minutes and serve.

similar.name: Avocado and Tomato Salsa (Great With Fish or Chicken)

name: Sweet Cherry Coke Barbecue Sauce
directions: combine all ingredients in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
reduce heat and simmer for 1 hour, stirring occasionally.

name: Easy Apple Orange Cranberry Sauce
directions: Sort the fresh cranberries, discarding any soft ones.
Add dried cranberries, apples, cinnamon and cloves.
In a saucepan over high heat, combine the orange extract, sugar and the water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and cover the pan partially. Simmer gently, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens, the apple is tender and the cranberries have burst, 10 to 15 minutes.
Transfer the cranberry sauce to a heatproof bowl and let cool for 1 hour before serving or cover and refrigerate.

similar.name: Cranberry Sauce With Dried Cherries and Cloves

name: Garlic-Chive Mayonnaise
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Allow the mayo to rest in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour so the flavors blend.

name: Cheese Sauce
directions: In a small saucepan melt butter. Stir in flour, salt and pepper until the mix becomes bubbly. Stir in milk all at once and whisk. Stir until thickened and bubbly then add cheese and stir again.

name: Easy Sweet and Sour Sauce
directions: Combine first 6 ingredients in a small saucepan on low-medium heat.
Stir often, until sugar dissolves.
Add habanero sauce if using.
Serve!

similar.name: Pineapple Sweet and Sour Stir Fry Sauce

name: Spicy Red Pepper Sauce
directions: In a blender or mini food processor, combine the bell pepper, hot sauce, zest, and 4 Tb mayonnaise.  Blend until smooth.  Cover and refrigerate until ready to serve.

name: Remoulade Sauce - Creole Style
directions: Put all of the ingredients into a blender or food processor and process until smooth, approximately 30 seconds.
Spoon the mixture into an airtight container and refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving. The sauce will keep in the fridge up to 1 week.

name: Spicy Cambodian Dipping Sauce
directions: Put the garlic, chilies, lime juice, sugar and water into a blender and whiz until you get a nice paste. Add the fish sauce and stir. Beat in the ground peanuts.

There are many variations to this sauce, and the beauty of making it yourself is that you can adjust the taste to your liking. Add more fish sauce if you want it to be saltier, or more water for the opposite effect. Add more sugar if you like it sweet and vary the amount of lime juice depending on how sour you want it .
There are no hard and fast rules here. The important thing is that you enjoy the sauce. Just make sure the basic ingredients are included in the recipe.

name: Holiday Cranberry-Pineapple Sauce
directions: Combine pineapple juice, water, nutmeg, and sugar and simmer over low heat until sugar is melted.
Stir in the whole berries and crushed pineapple and increase the heat to medium-high. Stir occasionally, until berries have burst and the sauce begins to thicken. (5-10 minutes).
Remove from heat and stir in cinnamon. Cool in refrigerator. Can be made the day before the holiday meal.

name: Sweet Pineapple Cranberry Sauce
directions: Put the sugar into your saucepan.
Zest the orange over the sugar.
Cut the orange in half and squeeze the juice into a measuring cup. You want approximately 1/2 cup of orange juice. Discard the rinds/pulp.

Place a mesh strainer over your measuring cup and dump in the crushed pineapple.  Use the back of a spoon to press the pineapple until you have gotten 1/2 cup of pineapple juice (making 1 full cup including the orange juice).
Set aside half of the strained pineapple, and discard or save the rest.
Dump the cup of juice into the saucepan with the sugar and orange zest. Stir well.
Rinse your cranberries in a pot of water. Discard any sinkers and overripe (mushy) berries.
Put your rinsed berries into your saucepan with the sugar, juice, and orange zest.

Bring everything to a boil over medium heat, stirring frequently.  Continue to boil (and frequently stir) for about 20 minutes. This is much longer than it takes for all the berries to pop, but it thickens the sauce.
Remove the sauce from heat and stir in the pineapple that you set aside.
Season with a couple small shakes (1/8 teaspoon?) of pumpkin pie spice and some generous shakes (1/4 teaspoon?) of ground cinnamon.
Refrigerate overnight. The warm sauce might taste bitter, but the cold sauce shouldn't.

name: Tournament BBQ Sauce
directions: combine all stuff together in saucepan.
put on med-high until sauce consistency.

name: BBQ Sauce - Standard
directions: put everything together into a pot.
simmer until thickened.
put on desired food or use as a dip.

name: Basic Cheddar Sauce
directions: Melt butter in small sauce pan until it stops bubbling.  Add the flour, stir until fully incorporated. Cook over medium heat until the roux browns slightly.
Add the milk slowly while whisking continually. Cook and stir over medium high heat until the sauce bubbles, then remove from heat.
Add the cheese & mustard powder, stir gently until the cheese melts and incorporates completely.  Season to taste.

name: Asian Barbecue Sauce
directions: Combine the first 5 ingredients in a small saucepan.  Bring to a boil; remove from heat.
Stir in the chili puree.
Use as a basting sauce for chicken, beef, or pork.

name: Sweet Dilly Tartar Sauce
directions: In small mixing bowl mix all ingredients.
Refrigerate, the longer the better, before serving. It can stay fresh in the refrigerator up to 3 - 4 days.

similar.name: My Secret Dill Sauce for Fish and Seafood

name: Black Bean and Corn Salsa
directions: Mix.
Refridgerate for 24 hours before use to let the flavors meld.
Discard after 4 days.
